content.body: Waves transfer energy without transferring matter. When two waves meet, they superimpose. Constructive interference increases amplitude; destructive reduces it.

Wave speed: v = fλ
Period: T = 1/f

Constructive interference: path difference = nλ
Destructive interference: path difference = (n+½)λ

Young's fringes: fringe spacing y = λD/d

content.title: Young's Double Slit
content.body:
λ = 600nm, D = 1.5m, d = 0.3mm
Fringe spacing y = λD/d
y = (600×10⁻⁹ × 1.5) / (0.3×10⁻³)
y = 3×10⁻³ m = 3mm

questions.question: A wave has frequency 200 Hz and wavelength 1.5m. Its speed is:
Constructive interference occurs when path difference is:
questions.explanation: Whole-number wavelength path difference gives constructive interference

questions.question: Increasing the slit separation d in Young's experiment:
questions.explanation: y = λD/d — fringe spacing decreases as d increases

questions.question: What type of waves can show polarisation?
questions.explanation: Polarisation only occurs in transverse waves (e.g. light, not sound)

questions.question: In a stationary wave, nodes are points of:
questions.explanation: Nodes are fixed points of zero displacement in a standing wave
